title: Split edge-vs-origin logic deliberately
description: Put only fast, stateless, request-shaping logic at the edge; keep stateful and heavy work at the origin.
body: Use when deciding where a piece of logic runs. Do not move database-backed or stateful logic to the edge — the edge has no consistent shared state and high fan-out. Actions: place auth checks, redirects, header rewrites, A/B routing, and cache-key normalization at the edge; keep transactions and writes at the origin; record why each piece lives where it does. Validation signals include reduced origin load and unchanged correctness. Risk boundary: stateful logic at the edge causes inconsistency across PoPs — confirm statelessness before edge placement.
